reviewSummary.summary: Core login controls are present but the draft artifact contains duplicate elements, weak naming, brittle locators, and lacks observable authentication error/validation elements needed for negative test verification.

issues.message: Two separate elements represent the same password field (xpath placeholder locator and id=password locator). This creates ambiguity for downstream resource and keyword generation.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep a single password textbox element using the stronger locator id=password and remove the duplicate placeholder-based locator.

issues.message: Element name uses inconsistent wording and underscore placement compared to other elements and approved workflow naming patterns.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename to username_textbox for consistent semantic naming aligned with workflow context and downstream resource usage.

issues.message: Element name 'password' is too generic and does not reflect the control type.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename to password_textbox to clearly represent the UI control.

issues.message: Locator uses placeholder text which can change during UI redesign or localization.
issues.suggested_fix: Prefer the stable attribute locator xpath=//input[@name='ion-input-0'] derived from the DOM attributes.

issues.message: Locator relies partly on visible text 'home', but the DOM indicates the icon is inside a shadow DOM and may not expose this text reliably.
issues.suggested_fix: Use icon attribute based locator such as xpath=//ion-fab-button[.//ion-icon[@aria-label='home']] for more reliable targeting.

issues.message: Locator depends on text matching 'arrow back' which may not be consistently exposed in the rendered DOM.
issues.suggested_fix: Use icon-based locator xpath=//ion-fab-button[.//ion-icon[@aria-label='arrow back']] to match the intended navigation control.

issues.message: Password field is classified as textbox rather than password input type.
issues.suggested_fix: Classify the element as type 'password' to reflect masked input behavior and support correct keyword generation.

issues.message: Workflow validations mention 'Sign in failed with invalid or incorrect credentials', but no authentication error message element or validation indicator is present in the artifact.
issues.suggested_fix: Identify and include the login failure message element (e.g., authentication_error_message or login_error_banner) so negative tests can verify actual error feedback instead of only confirming the page did not navigate.

issues.message: Locator depends on visible button text which may change with UI text updates.
issues.suggested_fix: Prefer a stable attribute if available (e.g., button role, id, or specific class) while keeping the current locator as fallback if no stronger attribute exists.

approved_elements.name: username_textbox
approved_elements.description: Username input field for entering login credentials

approved_elements.name: password_textbox
approved_elements.description: Password input field with masked characters
